essence: Explores how drugs interact with the body to develop safer, more effective treatments that improve human health.

identity.title: Pharmacologist
identity.summary: Studies drug actions, conducts laboratory research, analyses biological responses, and contributes to the development of new medicines and therapies.

salary.notes: Pharmaceutical industry roles, senior research positions, and specialist toxicology or clinical pharmacology posts can exceed £90k–£120k+.

qualifications: Degree in Pharmacology, Biomedical Science, or Related Field
Postgraduate Research (MSc or PhD) Often Required
Strong Understanding of Physiology, Biochemistry, and Drug Mechanisms

lifestyle.description: Pharmacologists work in research‑driven environments where curiosity, precision, and patience are essential. Days often involve designing experiments, analysing data, reviewing scientific literature, and collaborating with chemists, clinicians, and regulatory teams. Progress can be slow — breakthroughs may take months or years — but the impact is profound, contributing to new treatments, safer drugs, and advances in medical science. The work is structured, intellectually demanding, and suited to those who enjoy deep focus and scientific problem‑solving. It fits individuals who value discovery, evidence‑based thinking, and contributing to long‑term improvements in human health.
